# implement-roadmap-feature — IMPLEMENT

FRESH session — you have NO memory of previous iterations.
Read state from disk.

**Initial request**: settings-window
**Feedback from the previous failed iteration (if any)**: 

## Commit discipline

You commit AS YOU GO through implementation, **one commit per logical
block**, BEFORE the deterministic gate or the user validates anything.

**LANGUAGE — absolute rule: commit messages entirely in English.**

## Tooling

Prefer **Serena** (`mcp__serena__*`) if available.

## Setup

```bash
PLAN_FILE=$(ls roadmap/*.plan.md 2>/dev/null | head -1)
[ -n "$PLAN_FILE" ] || { echo 'ERROR: no roadmap/*.plan.md found'; exit 1; }
SLUG=$(basename "$PLAN_FILE" .plan.md)
EPIC_FILE="roadmap/$SLUG.md"
echo "=== $EPIC_FILE ==="
cat "$EPIC_FILE"
echo "=== $PLAN_FILE ==="
cat "$PLAN_FILE"
git log --oneline -20
git status
```

Read `CLAUDE.md`. If the epic touches Swift code, **also read the
five docs listed under "Swift code quality (mandatory)" in
`CLAUDE.md`** — mandatory before any Swift modification.

## Your mission this iteration

1. Identify what is already implemented (via `git log`) vs what
   remains in the plan. Iterating is OK — no need to do everything
   in one pass.
2. Implement the next logical block.
3. Write **solid tests**: happy path + edge cases + error cases.
   Don't wait for a downstream review to catch you.
4. Commit AS YOU GO, one commit per logical block:
   ```bash
   git add <files>
   git commit -m "<type>: <subject in English>" \
     -m "<WHY — retained trade-offs, discarded alternatives, technical decisions>"
   ```
   Types: `feat:`, `test:`, `refactor:`, `fix:`, `chore:`.
   NEVER describe the WHAT in the body — always the WHY.
5. Before declaring yourself ready, run build + tests yourself via
   bash to reduce round-trips on the gate.

## Exit criterion

When you are confident that:
- All relevant plan items are implemented
- Tests cover happy path + edge cases + errors
- Build + tests pass locally

Emit: <promise>IMPL_READY</promise>

The engine will then run `until_bash` as the DETERMINISTIC GATE.

=== roadmap/settings-window.md ===
# Settings window

## Goal

Give users a dedicated window to configure the application, accessible via a settings icon in the popover.

## Dependencies

None.

## Scope

- Add a cog (⚙) icon button in the popover that opens a native macOS settings window.
- The settings window hosts app-wide configuration options (exact settings to be defined during implementation; candidates include refresh interval, account management, and launch-at-login).
- The window follows macOS HIG for preferences windows (tabbed or single-pane depending on the number of settings).

**Out of scope**

- In-popover inline settings (settings live only in the dedicated window).
- Cloud sync of preferences.
- Per-vendor or per-account configuration beyond what the settings window exposes.

## Acceptance criteria

- Clicking the cog icon in the popover opens the settings window; the popover closes.
- The settings window can be dismissed independently; it does not block the menubar item.
- Changes made in the settings window take effect without restarting the application.

## Notes

- Exact list of configurable settings to be agreed on before implementation begins.
=== roadmap/settings-window.plan.md ===
---
title: Settings window
date: 2026-04-19
---

# Implementation plan — Settings window

## Overall approach

Introduce a first-class preferences layer (`AppPreferences`, an `@Observable @MainActor` store backed by `UserDefaults` and exposed to the lib via a protocol so tests can inject an in-memory backing) that every runtime-configurable behavior reads from. Wire the `UsagePoller` interval to that store, so changes take effect immediately (the poller actor observes a snapshot each tick — no restart required, fulfilling the "no restart" acceptance criterion). Replace the placeholder `Settings { EmptyView() }` scene in `AIUsagesTrackersApp.swift` with a real SwiftUI settings view, and open it from a new cog button in the popover footer using `NSApplication.showSettingsWindow(_:)` plus `NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps:)` (an `.accessory` app needs to be activated explicitly to bring the window to front). For the first pass we expose three settings: **refresh interval**, **launch at login** (via `SMAppService.mainApp`), and **log level** (moved from env var to user-facing). Account management and per-vendor config stay out of scope (epic).

Rejected alternative — a bespoke `NSWindow` built in `AppDelegate`: rejected because the SwiftUI `Settings` scene already provides HIG-correct chrome (tabbable, proper window title, correctly binds `Cmd+,`) and hooking into it is a one-liner via `showSettingsWindow(_:)`. We only give up programmatic control over window lifetime, which we do not need.

Rejected alternative — keeping the env-based log level only: rejected because the epic lists log verbosity under "app-wide configuration" naturally, and surfacing it in UI does not preclude keeping the env var as a developer override (env var wins when set, per existing `FileLogger` code path).

## Impacted areas

- `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Preferences/` — new module: `AppPreferences` protocol + `UserDefaultsAppPreferences` implementation + value objects for durations and log level choice.
- `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Scheduler/UsagePoller.swift` — read the refresh interval from the preferences store on each tick instead of holding a fixed `Duration`.
- `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Logging/Logger.swift` — consult preferences when the env var is not set, so the user-facing setting takes effect at runtime.
- `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/AIUsagesTrackersApp.swift` — replace the empty `Settings` scene with the real settings view.
- `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/SettingsView.swift` — new: root settings content (single-pane, tabbed only if we later split).
- `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/Settings/` — new: one view per settings section (`GeneralSettingsView`, `LoggingSettingsView`).
- `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/AppDelegate.swift` — instantiate the preferences store, inject it into poller + logger, and add a menubar/popover command to surface the window.
- `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/UsageDetailsView.swift` — add a cog button in the footer that closes the popover and opens the settings window.
- `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/LaunchAtLoginService.swift` — new: thin wrapper around `SMAppService.mainApp` exposing an injectable protocol.
- `AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/AIUsagesTrackersTests/AppPreferencesTests.swift` — new.
- `AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/AIUsagesTrackersTests/UsagePollerTests.swift` — extend to cover dynamic interval updates.
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — document the preferences layer and the settings-window activation dance.

## Phases and commits

### Phase 1 — Preferences infrastructure

**Goal**: land a tested, injectable preferences store that the rest of the app will consume, with no UI yet so failures stay localized.

#### Commit 1 — `feat(preferences): add AppPreferences store backed by UserDefaults`
- Files: `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Preferences/AppPreferences.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Preferences/AppPreferenceKeys.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Models/ValueObjects.swift` (extend)
- Changes:
  - Define a `public protocol AppPreferences: AnyObject, Observable, Sendable` with typed properties: `refreshInterval: RefreshInterval`, `launchAtLogin: Bool`, `logLevel: LogLevel`.
  - Add a `RefreshInterval` value object (struct wrapping `Duration` with `min = 30s`, `max = 30min`, `default = 180s`) in `ValueObjects.swift`. Follow `docs/SWIFT-VALUE-OBJECTS.md`: Codable, clamping init returning `Result`, `ExpressibleByIntegerLiteral` for test convenience.
  - Implement `UserDefaultsAppPreferences: @Observable @MainActor final class` reading/writing through an injected `UserDefaults` (defaults to `.standard`). Keys centralized in `AppPreferenceKeys` (enum with a `rawValue` string prefix to avoid collisions).
  - Provide a test double `InMemoryAppPreferences` in the lib (not the test target) so both production code (defaults when user defaults are absent) and tests can share it.
- Risk: UserDefaults reads on main thread only — the `@MainActor` constraint documents this. Cross-process writes (widgets) are out of scope.

#### Commit 2 — `test(preferences): cover AppPreferences read/write and clamping`
- Files: `AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/AIUsagesTrackersTests/AppPreferencesTests.swift`
- Changes:
  - Test that writes round-trip via a scratch `UserDefaults(suiteName:)`.
  - Test that `RefreshInterval` clamps out-of-range values (returns `.failure` below min / above max) per value-object rules.
  - Test that `@Observable` property reads fire observation on writes (use `withObservationTracking` directly).
  - Test defaults are returned when the suite is empty.
- Risk: `UserDefaults(suiteName:)` leaks across test runs — each test must call `removePersistentDomain(forName:)` in teardown.

### Phase 2 — Wire existing behaviors to preferences

**Goal**: make the poller and logger consume preferences without changing user-visible behavior (defaults match current hardcoded values).

#### Commit 3 — `refactor(poller): read refresh interval from AppPreferences`
- Files: `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Scheduler/UsagePoller.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/AppDelegate.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/AIUsagesTrackersTests/UsagePollerTests.swift`
- Changes:
  - `UsagePoller.init` takes an `AppPreferences` (default `UserDefaultsAppPreferences.shared`). The `interval` stored property is removed; each loop iteration reads `await MainActor.run { preferences.refreshInterval }` to pick up live changes.
  - `AppDelegate` constructs `UserDefaultsAppPreferences` once and passes it to both `UsagePoller` and the logger.
  - Test: inject an `InMemoryAppPreferences`, start the poller with interval `.seconds(30)`, mutate it to `.seconds(60)` mid-run, assert the next sleep uses the new value. Use `eventually()` poll helper per W3.
- Risk: The `await MainActor.run` call from inside the poller actor adds one hop per tick. Acceptable (polling cadence is minutes). Alternative (snapshot at `start()`) would break the "takes effect without restart" acceptance criterion.

#### Commit 4 — `refactor(logger): honor AppPreferences.logLevel when env var unset`
- Files: `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Logging/Logger.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/AIUsagesTrackersTests/LoggerTests.swift`
- Changes:
  - `FileLogger` gains an optional `AppPreferences` dependency. Resolution order: env var (`AI_TRACKER_LOG_LEVEL`) wins, then `preferences?.logLevel`, then the existing default.
  - Loggers singletons (`Loggers.app`, `Loggers.claude`) lazily read the shared preferences.
  - Test: set preferences to `.debug`, no env var, assert logger accepts debug lines; then set env var to `.error`, assert debug lines are dropped (env wins).
- Risk: Singleton coupling. Acceptable for now — the preferences store is effectively process-wide anyway.

### Phase 3 — Settings window UI

**Goal**: HIG-correct settings window driven by `AppPreferences`.

#### Commit 5 — `feat(settings): replace empty Settings scene with tabbed SettingsView`
- Files: `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/AIUsagesTrackersApp.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/SettingsView.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/Settings/GeneralSettingsView.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/Settings/LoggingSettingsView.swift`
- Changes:
  - `AIUsagesTrackersApp.body` replaces `Settings { EmptyView() }` with `Settings { SettingsView(preferences: AppDelegate.sharedPreferences) }`. The shared reference is exposed by the delegate as a `@MainActor static` so the Settings scene (constructed before `applicationDidFinishLaunching` runs) picks it up after init.
  - `SettingsView` is a `TabView` with a "General" tab (refresh interval `Slider` + numeric `TextField`, launch-at-login `Toggle`) and a "Logging" tab (log-level `Picker`). Uses SwiftUI `Form` with `.formStyle(.grouped)` to match macOS 14 conventions.
  - Bindings write straight through to the `AppPreferences` store — no apply/cancel button, matching macOS HIG for preferences.
- Risk: `Settings` scene is constructed eagerly but its content view is only instantiated when shown, so the shared-preferences lookup is safe. Double-check by running the app with the window closed — there must be no AppKit allocation until the user opens it.

#### Commit 6 — `feat(settings): open settings window from popover cog button`
- Files: `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/UsageDetailsView.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/AppDelegate.swift`
- Changes:
  - Add a cog button (`Image(systemName: "gearshape")`) in the footer, between the "AI Usages Tracker" label and the refresh button. On tap: call `onOpenSettings` closure supplied by the delegate.
  - `AppDelegate.openSettings()` performs, in order: `popover.performClose(nil)`, `NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)`, and `NSApp.sendAction(Selector(("showSettingsWindow:")), to: nil, from: nil)` (macOS 14 replacement for the deprecated `showPreferencesWindow:` selector).
  - Add `keyboardShortcut(",", modifiers: .command)` on the cog button to match `Cmd+,`.
  - Wire the closure into `UsageDetailsView` via its initializer (same pattern as `onRefresh`, `onQuit`).
- Risk: `.accessory` activation policy keeps the app out of the Dock, but `NSApp.activate` still focuses the settings window. If the user switches apps while the window is open, the window is dismissed by the system (expected HIG behavior for accessory apps).

### Phase 4 — Launch at login

**Goal**: the launch-at-login toggle actually works.

#### Commit 7 — `feat(settings): implement launch-at-login via SMAppService`
- Files: `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/LaunchAtLoginService.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/Settings/GeneralSettingsView.swift`, `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/AppDelegate.swift`
- Changes:
  - `LaunchAtLoginService` wraps `SMAppService.mainApp`, exposing `isEnabled: Bool { get }` and `setEnabled(_:) throws`. An injectable protocol `LaunchAtLoginManaging` lets us stub in tests.
  - `GeneralSettingsView`'s toggle now binds through the service: on toggle, call `register()` / `unregister()`, then update the preferences store only on success. On failure, surface the error via `Alert`.
  - `AppDelegate` reconciles preferences and service state at launch: if `preferences.launchAtLogin != service.isEnabled`, the service state wins (user may have disabled the app in System Settings > Login Items) and preferences are updated.
- Risk: `SMAppService` requires the app to be signed with a valid Team ID for production use. For unsigned dev builds, register/unregister silently no-ops; we log at `.warning` when unregistered status is unexpected. Does not block the feature.

### Phase 5 — Documentation and closure

**Goal**: reflect the new layer in the architecture doc; keep docs focused, no drift-prone lists.

#### Commit 8 — `docs(architecture): describe preferences layer and settings window`
- Files: `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`
- Changes:
  - Add a "Preferences" section explaining the `AppPreferences` role (single source of truth for user-adjustable runtime behavior, injectable) and naming the two consumers (poller cadence, logger verbosity) in terms of responsibilities rather than symbols.
  - Mention the settings window activation pattern (`.accessory` app must call `NSApp.activate` before `showSettingsWindow:`).
- Risk: none.

## Validation

Run from the repo root:

```bash
cd AIUsagesTrackers
swift build                      # SwiftLint plugin runs: E1/E2/W1/W3/W4/W5 must stay green
swift test                       # all tests pass, including the two new suites
```

Manual acceptance (mirrors the criteria in `roadmap/settings-window.md`):

1. Launch the app. Click the menu bar icon → popover shows. Click the cog button → popover closes, settings window appears, menu bar icon remains clickable.
2. Change **refresh interval** from 180s to 60s. Observe in `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/app.log` that the next poll tick fires on the new cadence without a restart.
3. Change **log level** to `debug` (env var unset). New debug lines appear in the logs immediately.
4. Toggle **launch at login** on; verify the entry appears in System Settings > General > Login Items. Toggle off; entry disappears.
5. Dismiss the settings window with `Cmd+W` or the close button — the menu bar icon still responds to clicks.
6. Open the settings window a second time via the cog button — no crash, fields reflect the previously-saved values.

### Phase 4 — Launch at login

**Goal**: the launch-at-login toggle actually works.

### Phase 5 — Documentation and closure

**Goal**: reflect the new layer in the architecture doc; keep docs focused, no drift-prone lists.

- Files: `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`
- Changes:

## Comprehensive Swift macOS App Codebase Exploration Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the AI Usages Trackers macOS application codebase. Here's the detailed findings:

### 1. Directory Structure

**Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/** (Core Library)
- `Connectors/` - API connectors for fetching usage data
- `FileWatcher/` - File system monitoring for usage changes
- `Helpers/` - Utility functions (usage computations, formatting)
- `Logging/` - Logging infrastructure
- `Models/` - Domain models and value objects
- `Persistence/` - File I/O and data persistence
- `Scheduler/` - Polling mechanism
- `Store/` - State management

**Sources/App/** (Application)
- `Views/` - SwiftUI views for UI
- `AIUsagesTrackersApp.swift` - App entry point
- `AppDelegate.swift` - Application lifecycle and menu bar integration
- `AppPidGuard.swift` - Single-instance enforcement
- `MenuBarLabelRenderer.swift` - Menu bar icon rendering

**Tests/AIUsagesTrackersTests/** (14 test files)
- Comprehensive test coverage for all major components
- Test helpers and mocks

### 2. Key Files and Contents

#### **Package.swift**
- Swift Package Manager manifest (v6.0)
- macOS 14+ minimum deployment
- SwiftLint build tool plugin for code quality
- Targets: AIUsagesTrackersLib (library), AIUsagesTrackers (executable), AIUsagesTrackersTests

#### **AIUsagesTrackersApp.swift**
Simple SwiftUI app entry point with `NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor` pattern. Currently has empty `Settings` scene placeholder that will be replaced with actual settings UI.

#### **AppDelegate.swift**
Core application lifecycle manager handling:
- PID guard to ensure single instance
- Popover setup with scrollable `UsageDetailsView`
- Status item (menu bar) setup with `MenuBarLabelRenderer`
- Observation tracking to keep menu bar synchronized with store state
- Component lifecycle management (poller, account monitor, file watcher)

#### **UsageDetailsView.swift**
Scrollable popover displaying usage data with:
- LazyVStack of `AccountCardView` entries
- Refresh button with progress indicator
- Quit button
- Footer showing "AI Usages Tracker" label
- Keyboard shortcuts: `r` for refresh, `q` for quit

#### **UsagePoller.swift**
Actor-based polling system:
- Configurable interval (default 180 seconds)
- Concurrent connector fetching
- Smart caching - skips fresh data
- Refresh state tracking for UI feedback
- Error resilience

#### **Logger.swift**
Multi-file logging system:
- Rotating file logs (5MB max before rotation)
- Environment variable control (`AI_TRACKER_LOG_LEVEL`)
- Four log levels: debug, info, warning, error
- Separate logs for app and connector
- Thread-safe via dispatch queue

#### **ValueObjects.swift**
Strong-typed value objects using RawRepresentable pattern:
- `AccountEmail` - Email address wrapper
- `ISODate` - ISO 8601 datetime with lazy parsing
- `Vendor` - AI vendor identifier (e.g., "claude")
- `MetricKind` - Forward-compatible metric type discriminator
- `UsagePercent` - 0-100 percentage value
- `DurationMinutes` - Time duration in minutes

#### **UsageModels.swift**
Domain model hierarchy:
- `UsagesFile` - Root file structure
- `VendorUsageEntry` - Per-account usage data with metrics
- `UsageError` - Error tracking with timestamp
- `UsageMetric` - Polymorphic metrics (timeWindow, payAsYouGo, unknown for forward compatibility)

#### **UsagesFileManager.swift**
Actor-based file persistence:
- flock-based synchronization for multi-process safety
- Atomic writes to prevent corruption
- Smart merging preserving errors alongside good metrics
- Location: `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/usages.json`

#### **UsagesFileWatcher.swift**
Hybrid file monitoring:
- FS events (DispatchSource) primary mechanism
- Polling fallback (30s default) for network mounts
- Deduplication by modification date
- Inode tracking to detect atomic file replacement

#### **ClaudeCodeConnector.swift**
Claude AI usage fetching:
- OAuth token retrieval from macOS Keychain
- API endpoint: `https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage`
- Fetches 5-hour session and weekly metrics
- Per-model Sonnet/Opus weekly metrics (optional)
- Sub-second precision normalization for timestamps
- Sensitive field masking in logs

#### **ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor.swift**
Account switching detection:
- Monitors `~/.claude.json` for active account changes
- 15-second polling interval
- Updates file's `isActive` flag when account switches
- Triggers forced poll on account change

#### **UsageStore.swift**
Observable store for UI:
- Displays formatted menu bar text (e.g., "S 48% 2h | W 7% 6d")
- Countdown refresh every 60 seconds to update remaining time
- Consumption tier calculation
- Forward-compatible unknown metric handling
- Menu bar segments with colored dots per tier

#### **RefreshState.swift**
Refresh progress tracking:
- Tracks which (vendor, account) pairs are fetching
- Enables per-account loading spinners in UI

#### **AppPidGuard.swift**
Single-instance enforcement:
- PID file at `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/app.pid`
- Signal handlers for graceful SIGTERM/SIGINT cleanup
- Stale PID detection

#### **MenuBarLabelRenderer.swift**
Menu bar icon rendering:
- Rasterizes segments to NSImage (not template)
- Per-tier colored dots (green→blue→yellow→orange→red→purple)
- Adapts text color to menu bar appearance (light/dark)
- Font: menu bar system font, 18pt height

#### **View Files**
- `AccountCardView.swift` - Displays account with all metrics, active badge, refresh spinner
- `TimeWindowMetricRow.swift` - Time-window metric with gauge bar, percentage, remaining time
- `PayAsYouGoMetricRow.swift` - Pay-as-you-go metric with currency formatting
- `GaugeBar.swift` - Horizontal progress bar with theoretical consumption marker
- `ConsumptionTierColor.swift` - Tier→Color mapping for UI

#### **UsageComputations.swift**
Computation helpers:
- `formatRemainingTime()` - Compact countdown (e.g., "2d 5h 13m")
- `theoreticalFraction()` - Expected progress through time window
- `formatResetDate()` - Context-aware date formatting
- `ConsumptionTier` enum with 6 severity levels (comfortable→exhausted)
- `consumptionRatio()` - Actual vs. theoretical usage comparison
- `consumptionTier()` - Maps ratio to severity
- Entry sorting and vendor grouping helpers

### 3. Preferences/Settings Infrastructure (Planned)

The codebase includes detailed **settings-window.plan.md** outlining the implementation strategy for a preferences layer that does NOT YET EXIST. The plan specifies:

**New Preferences Layer (To Be Implemented)**
- `AppPreferences` protocol + `UserDefaultsAppPreferences` implementation
- Backing via `UserDefaults` (mainApp domain)
- Value object: `RefreshInterval` (30s–30min, default 180s)
- Injected into: `UsagePoller`, `FileLogger`, and new `LaunchAtLoginService`

**Three User-Facing Settings (Planned)**
1. **Refresh Interval** - Slider + numeric field, takes effect without restart
2. **Launch at Login** - Toggle using `SMAppService.mainApp`
3. **Log Level** - Picker with env-var override support

**Settings Window (To Be Implemented)**
- Replaces current empty `Settings { EmptyView() }`
- macOS HIG-compliant tabbed view
- Cog button in popover footer to open settings (closes popover)
- Uses `NSApp.showSettingsWindow(_:)` after `NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps:)`

### 4. Test Structure

Comprehensive test coverage with 12 test files:
- `AppPreferencesTests.swift` (planned)
- `ClaudeActiveAccountMonitorTests.swift` - Account monitor lifecycle
- `ClaudeCodeConnectorTests.swift` - Token, response parsing, error handling
- `ConsumptionTierTests.swift` - Tier calculation and bounds
- `EventuallyHelperTests.swift` - Async polling utilities
- `LoggerTests.swift` - Log rotation, levels, formatting
- `UsageComputationsTests.swift` - Time formatting, tier logic
- `UsageModelsTests.swift` - Codable round-trip, metrics
- `UsagePollerTests.swift` - Polling, connector errors, caching
- `UsagesFileManagerTests.swift` - Persistence, merging, locking
- `UsagesFileWatcherTests.swift` - FS event, polling, inode detection
- `UsageStoreTests.swift` - Formatting, countdown refresh, observation

**Test Infrastructure**
- Modern Swift Testing framework
- Shared helpers: `eventually()` async polling, `absenceConfirmationDelay` timing
- Test doubles: `MockConnector`, `MockFileWatcher`, `FixedClock`, `MutableClock`
- Isolated temp directories per test

### 5. Architecture Highlights

**Actor-Based Concurrency**
- `UsagePoller` - Async polling with configurable connectors
- `ClaudeCodeConnector` - API fetching with OAuth token management
- `ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor` - File monitoring with account change callbacks
- `UsagesFileManager` - Serialized file I/O with flock coordination

**Observation Pattern**
- `UsageStore` uses `@Observable` for reactive UI updates
- Menu bar updates via `withObservationTracking` re-arming
- `RefreshState` tracks in-flight requests

**File-Based Synchronization**
- Central file: `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/usages.json`
- Advisory flock on separate `usages.json.lock`
- Atomic writes to prevent corruption across processes
- Hybrid polling (FS events + 30s timer) for network robustness

**Forward Compatibility**
- Unknown metric types preserved via `.unknown(String)` case
- Unknown log levels decodable without errors
- API versioning via `anthropic-beta` header

**Error Resilience**
- Connector errors don't stop polling
- Previous metrics preserved alongside error state
- File read failures return empty file (not crash)

### 6. Build & Configuration

- **Swift version**: 6.0+
- **Minimum OS**: macOS 14
- **SwiftLint**: Plugin-based enforcement during build
- **Package structure**: Single package, two targets (lib + executable)
- **Keychain**: "Claude Code-credentials" service for OAuth tokens
- **Logging**: `AI_TRACKER_LOG_LEVEL` env var override for log level

This is a production-quality macOS utility with strong separation of concerns, comprehensive testing, and a planned preferences system for user customization.

1	import AppKit
2	import SwiftUI
3	import AIUsagesTrackersLib
4	
5	@MainActor
6	final class AppDelegate: NSObject, NSApplicationDelegate {
7	    private var poller: UsagePoller?
8	    private var accountMonitor: ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor?
9	    private var pidGuard: AppPidGuard?
10	    private var usageStore: UsageStore?
11	    private var refreshState: RefreshState?
12	    private var statusItem: NSStatusItem?
13	    private var popover: NSPopover?
14	    private var appearanceObserver: NSKeyValueObservation?
15	
16	    func applicationDidFinishLaunching(_ notification: Notification) {
17	        NSApplication.shared.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
18	
19	        let home = FileManager.default.homeDirectoryForCurrentUser.path
20	        let guard_ = AppPidGuard(cacheDir: "\(home)/.cache/ai-usages-tracker")
21	        do {
22	            try guard_.acquire()
23	        } catch AppPidGuardError.alreadyRunning(let pid, _) {
24	            let alert = NSAlert()
25	            alert.messageText = "AI Usages Tracker is already running"
26	            alert.informativeText = "Another instance is active (PID \(pid)). Use the menu bar icon to quit it before launching a new one."
27	            alert.runModal()
28	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
29	            return
30	        } catch {
31	            let alert = NSAlert()
32	            alert.messageText = "Failed to start AI Usages Tracker"
33	            alert.informativeText = error.localizedDescription
34	            alert.runModal()
35	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
36	            return
37	        }
38	        pidGuard = guard_
39	
40	        let fileManager = UsagesFileManager.shared
41	        let refreshState = RefreshState()
42	        let poller = UsagePoller(
43	            connectors: [ClaudeCodeConnector()],
44	            fileManager: fileManager,
45	            refreshState: refreshState
46	        )
47	        let accountMonitor = ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor(
48	            fileManager: fileManager,
49	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak poller] _ in
50	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
51	            }
52	        )
53	        self.poller = poller
54	        self.accountMonitor = accountMonitor
55	        self.refreshState = refreshState
56	
57	        let usagesPath = fileManager.filePath
58	        let fileWatcher = UsagesFileWatcher(path: usagesPath)
59	        let store = UsageStore(fileWatcher: fileWatcher)
60	        self.usageStore = store
61	
62	        setupStatusItem(store: store, refreshState: refreshState)
63	        trackStoreChanges(store: store)
64	
65	        Task {
66	            store.start()
67	            await poller.start()
68	            await accountMonitor.start()
69	        }
70	    }
71	
72	    func applicationWillTerminate(_ notification: Notification) {
73	        quit()
74	    }
75	
76	    // MARK: - Status item
77	
78	    private func setupStatusItem(store: UsageStore, refreshState: RefreshState) {
79	        let item = NSStatusBar.system.statusItem(withLength: NSStatusItem.variableLength)
80	        statusItem = item
81	
82	        let popover = NSPopover()
83	        popover.behavior = .transient
84	        popover.contentSize = NSSize(width: 320, height: 400)
85	        popover.contentViewController = NSHostingController(
86	            rootView: UsageDetailsView(
87	                store: store,
88	                refreshState: refreshState,
89	                onRefresh: { [weak self] in
90	                    await self?.poller?.pollOnce(now: Date(), force: true)
91	                },
92	                onQuit: { [weak self] in
93	                    self?.quit()
94	                }
95	            )
96	        )
97	        self.popover = popover
98	
99	        if let button = item.button {
100	            button.target = self
101	            button.action = #selector(togglePopover(_:))
102	            // The menu bar appearance can change (wallpaper-driven tinting) without
103	            // the app's effectiveAppearance changing, so we observe the button itself.
104	            appearanceObserver = button.observe(\.effectiveAppearance, options: [.new]) { [weak self] _, _ in
105	                Task { @MainActor in self?.refreshStatusItemImage() }
106	            }
107	        }
108	
109	        refreshStatusItemImage()
110	    }
111	
112	    private func refreshStatusItemImage() {
113	        guard let store = usageStore, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
114	        let isDark = button.effectiveAppearance.bestMatch(from: [.darkAqua, .aqua]) == .darkAqua
115	        let image = MenuBarLabelRenderer.render(
116	            segments: store.menuBarSegments,
117	            fallbackText: store.menuBarText,
118	            isDarkMenuBar: isDark
119	        )
120	        button.image = image
121	    }
122	
123	    @objc private func togglePopover(_ sender: Any?) {
124	        guard let popover, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
125	        if popover.isShown {
126	            popover.performClose(sender)
127	        } else {
128	            popover.show(relativeTo: button.bounds, of: button, preferredEdge: .minY)
129	            popover.contentViewController?.view.window?.makeKey()
130	        }
131	    }
132	
133	    // MARK: - Observation
134	
135	    /// Re-arms `withObservationTracking` after every change so the status item
136	    /// keeps mirroring the store. Tracking fires exactly once per registration.
137	    private func trackStoreChanges(store: UsageStore) {
138	        withObservationTracking {
139	            _ = store.menuBarSegments
140	            _ = store.menuBarText
141	        } onChange: { [weak self] in
142	            Task { @MainActor in
143	                guard let self, let store = self.usageStore else { return }
144	                self.refreshStatusItemImage()
145	                self.trackStoreChanges(store: store)
146	            }
147	        }
148	    }
149	
150	    // MARK: - Quit
151	
152	    private func quit() {
153	        let pollerRef = poller
154	        let monitorRef = accountMonitor
155	        Task {
156	            await pollerRef?.stop()
157	            await monitorRef?.stop()
158	        }
159	        usageStore?.stop()
160	        pidGuard?.release()
161	        NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
162	    }
163	}

21	    func respectsMinLevel() {
22	        let path = makeTempPath()
23	        let logger = FileLogger(filePath: path, minLevel: .warning)
24	        logger.log(.debug, "should not appear")
25	        logger.log(.info, "should not appear")
26	        logger.log(.warning, "should appear")
27	        logger.waitForPendingWrites()
28	        let content = try! String(contentsOfFile: path, encoding: .utf8)
29	        #expect(!content.contains("should not appear"))
30	        #expect(content.contains("should appear"))
31	    }

37	        logger.log(.error, "test message")
38	        logger.waitForPendingWrites()
39	        let content = try! String(contentsOfFile: path, encoding: .utf8)
40	        #expect(content.contains("[ERROR]"))
41	        #expect(content.contains("test message"))
42	        #expect(content.contains("[20")) // ISO8601 year prefix
43	    }

46	    func logLevelFromString() {
47	        #expect(LogLevel.from(string: "debug") == .debug)
48	        #expect(LogLevel.from(string: "DEBUG") == .debug)
49	        #expect(LogLevel.from(string: "warning") == .warning)
50	        #expect(LogLevel.from(string: "warn") == .warning)

56	    @Test("LogLevel comparison works")
